- 2025-02-12 Tomoko updated SLA targets after infra sizing call.

title: Activation Gap Intervention Runbook
preview:
Purpose:
This runbook defines a lightweight, operationally-focused health scoring approach for identifying customers that have an "activation gap"—i.e., accounts that have completed initial onboarding but show falling or inconsistent product activity that puts renewal at risk. The goal is to produce an actionable per-customer "Activation Score" and a short, prioritized intervention plan for CSMs and SEs.

Background and assumptions:
- We treat "activation" as a sustained, repeatable pattern of product usage following onboarding (not just a single success event).
- This worksheet is intended to be computed weekly and surfaced in the CSM scoreboard used for QBR planning and renewal forecasting.
- Scores map to operational tiers (Green/Watch/Intervene) with clear, time-boxed actions.
- The model is deliberately simple and interpretable so CSMs can explain the reason for outreach to customers.

Signals considered (examples, prioritized):
1) Active API endpoints per customer (30d vs 90d delta) — measures breadth of product adoption.
2) Token/Call volume trend (per-seat normalized) — signal of sustained usage.
3) Feature adoption flags: embeddings, function-calling, streaming — binary flags (used in last 60d).
4) Failed requests % / latency regressions — quality-of-service issues can create churn risk.
5) New seats / seat churn (net delta in last 30d).
6) Support volume weighted by severity (P0/P1 elevated).
7) QBR / exec meeting cadence (days since last high-touch).
8) Customer-supplied quality signals (NPS, support CSAT, pilot success notes).
9) Billing anomalies (invoice disputes, payment failures).
10) Contract runway (months until renewal).

Scoring approach (normalized 0-100):
- Each signal is normalized to a 0–100 subscore. Recent deltas are emphasized: 60% weight to 30-day behavior, 40% to 90-day baseline.
- Weights (initial recommendation):
  - Usage trend (token volume per active seat): 28
  - Active endpoints delta: 18
  - Feature adoption breadth: 12
  - Support severity index: 12
  - QoS regressions (errors/latency): 10
  - Seat delta: 8
  - Payment or billing issues: 6
  - Days since last high-touch (inverted): 6
  (weights sum to 100)

Sample subscore normalization rules (illustrative):
- Usage trend subscore: 100 for >= +10% 30d/90d, 50 for stable (±5%), 0 for >= -20% decline. Linear interpolation between anchors.
- Active endpoints delta: 100 if +2 or more endpoints in 30d, 50 if unchanged, 0 if -2 endpoints.
- Feature breadth: 100 if 3+ major features used in last 60d, 60 if 2, 30 if 1, 0 if none.
- Support severity: 100 if 0 P1/P0 tickets in 30d, 50 if 1 P1, 0 if multiple unresolved P1/P0.
- QoS regressions: 100 if error rate < 0.1% and latency within SLA, degrade to 0 if error rate > 5% or repeated SLA misses.

Activation Score calculation example (compressed):
- Customer A: token trend -12% => usage subscore 20 (weighted 28 => 5.6), active endpoints -1 => subscore 20 (weighted 18 => 3.6), feature breadth 2 features => subscore 60 (weighted 12 => 7.2), support 1 P1 resolved => subscore 50 (weighted 12 => 6), QoS okay => subscore 80 (weighted 10 => 8), seat +0 => subscore 50 (weighted 8 => 4), billing clean => subscore 100 (weighted 6 => 6), last-touch 45 days => subscore 30 (weighted 6 => 1.8). Total = 42.2 => Activation Score 42 (rounded).

Operational tiers and thresholds (suggestion):
- Green (>= 70): Healthy. Continue standard cadences.
- Watch (50–69): Potentially slipping. Add watch triggers: bi-weekly check-ins, automated tips, validate any friction.
- Intervene (< 50): Active outreach: CSM + Solutions led triage within 7 days. Consider technical deep-dive + renewal risk layering.

Risk flag matrix (examples): immediate escalation if any of the following are true, regardless of score:
- Payment failure or open billing dispute older than 7 days.
- Repeated P0/P1 within 14 days.
- >30% decline in token calls month-over-month.
- Loss of critical feature (e.g., function-calling) that was key to the customer's workflow.

Action runbook (timeboxed):
- Intervene (Score < 50 or risk flag):
  1) Day 0: CSM sends empathy-first email acknowledging observed drop, requests quick sync.
  2) Day 1–3: If no response, SE does technical pulse (review logs, error trends, quota issues). Create a "pulse" doc and flag Support P1 if required.
  3) Day 3–7: 60-minute troubleshooting + action plan with owner + timeline. Share in ticket and update customer.
  4) Day 7–14: If usage not recovered or contract sensitive, schedule executive sponsorship call; prepare renewal impact brief for GTM.
  5) Post-recovery: capture root cause, update playbook with lessons and create a short-run automation if repeatable.

- Watch (50–69):
  1) CSM schedules a light check-in (15–30 minutes) to review product value and identify friction.
  2) Offer quick wins (optimizing request batching, advising on quantization/profile) to reduce friction/cost.
  3) Add to 30-day "watch" dashboard and re-evaluate.

- Green (>=70): No special action. Maintain scheduled QBR cadence.

CSM checklist for intervention call:
- Confirm whether usage decline is intentional (seasonality, budget pause) or unintentional.
- Share simple data points: token trend, endpoints active, errors observed.
- Confirm business metrics tied to product usage.
- Propose immediate next steps (pilot reconfiguration, SE triage, extended trial).

Signals to surface in the QBR deck (one slide):
- 90/30 trend for tokens & endpoints, top failed endpoints by error class, seat delta, feature adoption timeline, risk flags triggered, recommended interventions and expected impact. Keep slide to one chart + three bullets.

Automation & monitoring suggestions:
- Weekly cohort job to compute Activation Score and export top 200 watch/intervene accounts to CSM scoreboard.
- Alerting rules: score drop > 15 points week-over-week; any immediate risk flag events feed Slack #cs-escalations channel with prepopulated context.
- Add a lightweight Zendesk/Support integration to attach the latest "pulse" snapshot to tickets created from this workflow.

Sample short-term experiments (to validate model):
1) Reduce weight on endpoints and increase on token trend for 8 weeks to see if precision improves.
2) Run human review on 50 intervene cases: did outreach prevent churn? Track outcomes for model calibration.
3) A/B two intervention cadences (immediate SE + CSM vs. CSM-first) and compare recovery rates.

Open questions and notes:
- How do we treat accounts with very low seat counts but high cost per seat? Possible separate weighting or categorical override.
- Should we include usage quality signals (percentage of failed completions) into feature breadth instead of QoS bucket?
- Data availability: confirm that 30d/90d endpoint deltas and per-seat normalization are accessible in the analytics pipeline.

Next steps (owners & timeline):
- Data infra: validate required feeds (tokens, endpoints, support severity) by 2024-02-05 — owner: Priya K.
- CSM pilot: identify 25 target customers for pilot (mix of expansion and renewal) by 2024-02-12 — owner: Marcus L.
- Run pilot for 6 weeks, collect outcomes, and iterate weights — target: 2024-03-29 — owner: CS leadership.

Appendix: quick calibration cheat-sheet (how to explain score to a customer)
- "Your Activation Score is 0–100; it aggregates recent usage, feature breadth, and reliability signals. Numbers under 50 indicate we see a meaningful reduction in activity versus your baseline and we'd like to check in to make sure the product is delivering value.

Document history and edits:
- 2024-01-18 initial draft by Marcus Lin.
- 2024-02-09 updated weights after internal CS workshop (Priya Kapoor).
- 2024-03-22 added operational tiers and escalation steps (Sofia Chen).
